title: Expressing support for designation of March 14, 2025, as "National Pi Day".
plain_summary: This bill asks Congress to support making March 14, 2025 a special day called National Pi Day. Pi is an important number in math that equals about 3.14, which is why March 14th was chosen for the date. The bill says American students are not doing as well in math and science compared to students in other countries. It wants schools to use Pi Day as a fun way to help students learn more about math and get excited about it.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.

sections.title: Resolution Expressing Support for Pi Day
sections.text: This resolution supports making March fourteenth, twenty twenty-five a National Pi Day. Pi is a Greek letter that represents a special number in math. This number shows the relationship between how far it is around a circle compared to how far it is across the circle. Pi is a never-ending number that goes on forever without repeating the same pattern. Scientists have calculated Pi to more than two hundred and two trillion digits. Pi is very important in math, science, and engineering. Math and science education are very important for children. Being good at math, science, and engineering is necessary for our modern world. A twenty twenty-three international study found that American fourth and eighth grade students scored lower in math and science than students in countries like Singapore, South Korea, Taiwan, and Japan. Black and Hispanic students and students from poor schools had lower scores than average. Boys scored higher than girls in math and science, showing a gap between genders that starts early in school. The United States needs to improve math and science education for all students to prepare them for jobs in the twenty-first century. The National Science Foundation has been working to improve math and science education at all levels for seventy-five years. Math and science can be fun and interesting for children. Learning about Pi can be an exciting way to teach children about shapes and get them interested in science and math. Pi can be written as approximately three point one four, so March fourteenth is a good day for National Pi Day. The House of Representatives supports celebrating Pi Day around the world, recognizes the importance of the National Science Foundation's education programs, and encourages schools and teachers to use the day for activities that teach students about Pi and get them excited about studying math.
